description: Per-species transfer (tracks phylogenetic proximity) + coevolution-block delta (helps 8/13, viral-biased). [Publication-grade, rebuilt]
Transfer degrades with evolutionary distance; species with no relative sit at chance; no cross-kingdom transfer — the sharpest framing of the transfer result.
Original 3-panel balanced-transfer figure — superseded by figB_transfer.
Within-species vs leave-one-species-out on the original imbalanced 5-species benchmark (the 0.485 'chance' number later shown to be an artifact).
Per-species composition of the balanced 13-species benchmark (≤400 positives/species; pathogen-protein diversity by design).
Cross-system scorecard: significant in 3/8 systems; absolute-entropy panel separates real (SARS-CoV-2) from spurious high-fold (YpkA). [Publication-grade, rebuilt]
Original cross-system interface-variation figure (v1) — superseded by figA_crosssystem.
Deep bacterial isolate panels (86–800 seqs) that make the bacterial arm of the cross-system test meaningful.
Coverage of the 101,132-pair master interaction corpus — by source, species, evidence tier, and publication timeline.
Live database coverage of host–pathogen complexes: the viral (abundant structures, shallow proteomes) vs bacterial asymmetry that drives method choice.
Breakdown of the interaction corpus by primary source (IntAct/IMEx, structural record) and evidence type.
The 59-reference curated literature corpus organized by methodological theme.
Effective MSA depth (Neff/L) across all 16 partners — most alignments fall far below the DCA depth floor.
Homolog / alignment depth per partner in the pilot set.
Stage-1 prep: the experimental interface ground-truth set (146k residue contacts) and the MSA-ready sequence set (15,875 pairs).
Interface prediction from strain entropy (strong only for SARS-CoV-2) + balanced within-vs-cross benchmark. [Publication-grade, rebuilt]
Interface-prediction AUC per system + depth-robustness (YpkA fold inflates at low depth; anthrax flat).
Stage-2 interface prediction from entropy across systems.
Stage-2 signal strength vs strain-panel depth.

Six-panel Stage-1 synthesis over 8 structural complexes: which signal fires per complex, depth-necessary-not-sufficient, MSA-free ESM-2 (fold yes/interface no), arms-race selection, interface conservation, and takeaways.
The 8-pair signal map: interface size, homolog depth asymmetry, and shared-taxa=0 for 7/8 pairs.
CPU coevolution analysis (3 panels): MSA depth barrier, zero co-speciation overlap, and the DCA positive control on Ras/Rho.
The co-speciation pairing barrier visualized — host and pathogen homolog sets share no species.
DCA positive control: top couplings recover true 3D contacts on the Ras/Rho superfamily (proves the math works where depth exists).
Negative result: naive ESM-2 inter-protein coupling does not localize the interface (AUC 0.58, enrichment 1.01×).
Experimental interface contact maps for the two lead pilot complexes.
Interface contact maps (revised) across the structural systems.
Signal family 1 — intra-protein conservation & coupling patterns at the interface.
Signal family 2 — ESM-2 inter-chain coupling evaluation against the true interface.
Signal family 3 — interface positive selection / arms-race across partners.
Arms-race signature: interface residues evolve faster than the buried core in deep partners.
PLM masked-LM constraint as an arms-race proxy — host vs pathogen asymmetry.
Phylogenetic profiling: bacterial-partner co-occurrence across genomes recovers known family structure.
Coevolution coupling heatmaps for the analyzed complexes.
Signal-availability scorecard across 9 human–commensal PPI pilot pairs (separate commensal thread): inter-protein DCA undefined everywhere; intra-protein & arms-race present.
NEWEST: AlphaFold-3 host–pathogen pilot — complex confidence (iPTM), interface recovery vs PDB ground truth, and confidence-vs-accuracy, 8 of 11 pairs scored (iPTM 0.11–0.70, F1 0–0.63).
Pilot overview panel for the lead complexes.
Coevolution-feature ablation on the imbalanced benchmark (embeddings vs +coevo vs coevo-only).
Headline: SARS-CoV-2 concentrates strain variation at ACE2-contact residues — one signal, two baselines (4.2× within RBD, 9.75× vs whole Spike), perm p=1e-4. [Publication-grade, rebuilt]
The three tests of the new signal on SARS-CoV-2: cross-side coupling (null), strain-covariation→contact (null), interface-localized variation (strong).
Population×strain variation dataset: ACE2 (675 missense variants, interface invariant) vs RBD (per-residue strain entropy).
